symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent escalating falls (three in one week)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent falls in a frail elder define the syndrome and mandate multifactorial assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unsteady, wide-based, slow shuffling gait, worsening (carer-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A deteriorating gait pattern is a core, modifiable fall contributor and a target for physiotherapy assessment.
Proximal weakness reduces the ability to recover from a loss of balance; contributes to fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal lower-limb weakness, power 4/5, with intact sensation and reflexes and no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective proximal myopathic pattern with preserved sensation/reflexes; sealed clinician examination — the AI infers weakness from the patient's report, not a measured power grade.
Symmetrical weakness with no focal deficit argues against an acute stroke driving the falls.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies postural dizziness / near-faint on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of postural symptoms makes orthostatic hypotension an unlikely primary driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: No significant postural blood-pressure drop on measurement (135/80 lying to 130/78 standing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A formal clinician-supervised lying-standing BP with no drop excludes orthostatic hypotension; the AI cannot obtain a protocolised postural BP remotely.
Impaired vision degrades obstacle detection and is a standard modifiable fall contributor warranting formal acuity testing.
Sedative-hypnotic and anticholinergic burden is a leading, directly modifiable fall driver (Beers-criteria) — but reconciliation/deprescribing requires in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute right-knee swelling (video-observable) with refusal to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Swelling and pain after a fall; visible on video and reportable, but the injury cannot be characterised remotely.
Refusal to weight-bear after a fall is a red flag that mandates in-person examination and imaging to exclude fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Knee examination: full active/passive range of motion, no bony crepitus or point tenderness, intact straight-leg-raise, contusion/swelling over the prepatellar bursa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The reassuring exam (full ROM, no bony tenderness, intact SLR) that downgrades fracture probability is a sealed hands-on finding the AI cannot obtain.
In-person examination lowers, but does not fully exclude, fracture — imaging remains the definitive step.

symptoms.symptom_name: No palpitations and no loss of consciousness with the falls; regular pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of syncopal/palpitation features and a regular pulse make cardiac syncope an unlikely driver, though it is not fully excluded without cardiac monitoring.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no delirium, clear urine dipstick

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal temperature, no delirium and a clear dipstick argue against an infective precipitant; dipstick and temperature interpretation are clinician/facility findings, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Environmental hazards — cluttered room and faulty walker brakes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Modifiable environmental hazards are a directly actionable fall contributor (declutter, repair/replace the walker).

symptoms.symptom_name: Fear of falling with self-imposed activity restriction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fear of falling drives deconditioning and further fall risk — a target for physiotherapy and behavioural intervention.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Gait deterioration, proximal weakness, visual impairment, fall-precipitating polypharmacy and environmental hazards together constitute the multifactorial fall risk — each is individually modifiable at an in-person multidomain assessment.
A fall plus refusal to weight-bear on a swollen knee cannot be cleared remotely; imaging and in-person examination are required to exclude fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person examination and X-ray imaging of the right knee (and hip/pelvis if weight-bearing failure persists)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To characterise the knee injury and exclude an occult fracture in a frail faller who will not weight-bear — cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Structured multidomain falls assessment (gait/balance, Timed-Up-and-Go, postural BP, vision, cognition)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recurrent escalating falls require a formal in-person multifactorial risk assessment to target each modifiable contributor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person medication reconciliation with deprescribing review of the sedative-hypnotic and anticholinergic
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The polypharmacy fall drivers require a clinician/pharmacist reconciliation and supervised deprescribing — not a remote medication change.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBE, U&E, creatinine/eGFR, CRP) and urine MCS to screen reversible precipitants
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard screen for occult infection, anaemia, electrolyte and renal contributors to falls — requires a live lab the AI cannot access.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat cardiac 12-lead ECG / monitoring for arrhythmia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No syncopal or palpitation features and a regular pulse; routine cardiac monitoring is not warranted as a first step and should not be over-ordered by the AI.
